root_text.ud7.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud7.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā mahākaccāno bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya kāyagatāya satiyā ajjhattaṁ parimukhaṁ sūpaṭṭhitāya.
root_text.ud7.8:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahākaccānaṁ avidūre nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya kāyagatāya satiyā ajjhattaṁ parimukhaṁ sūpaṭṭhitāya.
root_text.ud7.8:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud7.8:4.3: No cassa no ca me siyā,
root_text.ud7.8:4.4: Na bhavissati na ca me bhavissati;

translation_text.ud7.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud7.8:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Mahākaccāna was sitting not far from the Buddha, cross-legged, with his body straight and mindfulness of the body well-established internally in his presence.
translation_text.ud7.8:2.1: The Buddha saw him meditating there.
translation_text.ud7.8:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud7.8:4.2: be established in the body, constant:
translation_text.ud7.8:4.3: ‘It might not be, and it might not be mine,
translation_text.ud7.8:4.4: It will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.ud7.8:4.5: Meditating stage by stage on that,
translation_text.ud7.8:4.6: in time they’d cross over clinging.”
